---
title: "MuData as standard data format"
description: "The single data object every component reads and writes, with converters at the edges."
order: 30
---

Every component reads and writes the same
[MuData](https://mudata.readthedocs.io/) object, modalities under `.mod`, cells
in `.obs`, features in `.var`. That shared format is why components snap together
without conversion, and is the foundation of OpenPipeline's
[modularity](modularity.qmd).

```
MuData
├─ .mod
│  ├─ rna  (AnnData)
│  │   ├─ .X          # expression matrix
│  │   ├─ .layers     # alternative matrices
│  │   ├─ .obs / .var # cell & feature annotation
│  │   └─ .obsm/.varm # embeddings, loadings
│  └─ prot (AnnData)
├─ .obs   # cross-modality cell annotation (e.g. sample_id)
└─ .var
```

MuData is the multimodal extension of [AnnData](https://anndata.readthedocs.io).
If you are new to it, read up on AnnData first, MuData is roughly a collection
of AnnData objects, one per modality, stored in the `.mod` attribute.

## Anatomy of the object

Each slot has a fixed meaning, and the same slots exist both per-modality (inside
each AnnData under `.mod`) and at the top level (across modalities):

- **`.mod`**: the associative array of AnnData objects, one per modality
  (`rna`, `prot`, `atac`, `vdj_t`, …).
- **`.X` and `.layers`**: the measurement matrices. Columns are variables
  (genes/features), rows are observations (cells). `.layers` holds alternative
  versions of the same matrix (e.g. raw counts vs. normalized).
- **`.var`**: metadata for the variables (annotation for the columns of `.X`).
  One row per feature.
- **`.obs`**: metadata for the observations (annotation for the rows of `.X`).
  One row per cell. At the top level, `.obs` holds cross-modality cell annotation
  such as `sample_id`.
- **`.varm` / `.obsm`**: multi-dimensional variable / observation annotations:
  key → matrix mappings aligned to features (`.varm`, e.g. PCA loadings) or cells
  (`.obsm`, e.g. embeddings like `X_pca`, `X_umap`).
- **`.uns`**: unstructured annotation, with no dimensional constraints (run
  parameters, colour maps, cluster metadata).

## Working with other formats

You do not have to leave your existing tools behind. OpenPipeline ships
**converter components** in the `convert` namespace that translate to and from
the common single-cell data formats, AnnData (H5AD), Seurat, TileDB, and 10x,
with spatial formats (SpatialData, SpatialExperiment) added by the
[`openpipeline_spatial`](ecosystem.qmd) package. The concept is simple: **convert
once at the edge of the pipeline; everything in between stays MuData.**

Browse the [`convert` namespace](../reference/index.qmd) in the reference for the
full list of converters and their arguments.
